## Summary
The string landscape refers to the vast number (~10⁵⁰⁰) of metastable vacuum states predicted by string theory, each with different effective low-energy physics. Combined with eternal inflation, this provides a framework where the observed cosmological constant is explained anthropically rather than dynamically.

## Evidence
Constructions in Type IIB string theory (KKLT, large volume scenarios) demonstrate the existence of de Sitter vacua with small positive cosmological constant, though the robustness of these constructions remains debated. The sheer number of vacua makes statistical predictions possible in principle, but the measure problem in eternal inflation undermines uniqueness of predictions.

## Significance
The landscape proposal is deeply provocative: it either represents the ultimate explanation for apparent fine-tuning in physics or constitutes a retreat from predictive science. Its testability through bubble collision signatures in the CMB or specific statistical predictions for coupling constants remains an active and contentious area of research.
